subject: Physics
q: A circular plate is rotating in horizontal plane, about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to the plate, with an angular velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ω</mi></math>. A person sits at the centre having two dumbbells in his hands. When he stretched out his hands, the moment of inertia of the system becomes triple. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> be the initial Kinetic energy of the system, then final Kinetic energy will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>E</mi><mi>x</mi></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is
question: A circular plate is rotating in horizontal plane, about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to the plate, with an angular velocity <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>ω</mi></math>. A person sits at the centre having two dumbbells in his hands. When he stretched out his hands, the moment of inertia of the system becomes triple. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>E</mi></math> be the initial Kinetic energy of the system, then final Kinetic energy will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>E</mi><mi>x</mi></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is
solution:
The kinetic energy is given by 

E=12Iω2=L22I

It is given that the new moment of inertia

 I'=3I 

Clearly, the final kinetic energy is

Ef=L22I'=L26I⇒Ef=E3

subject: Physics
q:
A solid cylinder of mass m is wrapped with an inextensible light string and, is placed on a rough inclined plane as shown in the figure. The frictional force acting between the cylinder and the inclined plane is:

[The coefficient of static friction, μs, is 0.4 ]
question:
A solid cylinder of mass m is wrapped with an inextensible light string and, is placed on a rough inclined plane as shown in the figure. The frictional force acting between the cylinder and the inclined plane is:

[The coefficient of static friction, μs, is 0.4 ]
solution:
Let's take solid cylinder is in equilibrium

T+f=mgsin60

TR-fR=0

Solving we get

T=freq=mgsinθ2

But limiting friction < required friction

μmgcos60°<mgsin60°2

∴ Hence cylinder will not remain in equilibrium

Hence f= kinetic

=μkN

=μkmgcos60°

=mg5

subject: Physics
q:
This question has Statement 1 and Statement 2. Of the four choices given after the Statements, choose the one that best describes the two Statements.
Statement 1: When moment of inertia $I$ of a body rotating about an axis with angular speed $\omega$ increases, its angular momentum $L$ is unchanged but the kinetic energy $K$ increases if there is no torque applied on it.
Statement 2: $L=I \omega$, kinetic energy of rotation $=\frac{1}{2} I \omega^2$
question:
This question has Statement 1 and Statement 2. Of the four choices given after the Statements, choose the one that best describes the two Statements.
Statement 1: When moment of inertia $I$ of a body rotating about an axis with angular speed $\omega$ increases, its angular momentum $L$ is unchanged but the kinetic energy $K$ increases if there is no torque applied on it.
Statement 2: $L=I \omega$, kinetic energy of rotation $=\frac{1}{2} I \omega^2$
options: Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is true, Statement 2 is not the correct explanation of Statement 1.
Statement 1 is false, Statement 2 is true.
Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is true, Statement 2 is correct explanation of the Statement 1.
Statement 1 is true, Statement 2 is false.
solution:
As $L=I \omega$ so $L$ increases with increase in $\omega$. Kinetic energy $_{\text {(rotational) }}$ depends on an angular velocity ' $\omega$ ' and moment of inertia of the body $I$.
i.e., $K \cdot E \cdot\left(\right.$ rotational $=\frac{1}{2} I \omega^2$

subject: Physics
q: Consider a thin uniform square sheet made of a rigid material. If its side is <math> <mi>a</mi> </math>, mass m and moment of inertia <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi></math> about one of its diagonals, then:
question: Consider a thin uniform square sheet made of a rigid material. If its side is <math> <mi>a</mi> </math>, mass m and moment of inertia <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>I</mi></math> about one of its diagonals, then:
solution:
In a uniform square plate due to symmetry moment of Inertia about all the axis passing through centre and lying in the plane of the plate is same.

∴    Idiagonal=ma212

subject: Physics
q: A solid sphere and a hollow cylinder roll up without slipping on same inclined plane with same initial speed $v$. The sphere and the cylinder reaches upto maximum heights $h_1$ and $h_2$, respectively, above the initial level. The ratio $h_1: h_2$ is $\frac{n}{10}$. The value of $n$ is______.
question: A solid sphere and a hollow cylinder roll up without slipping on same inclined plane with same initial speed $v$. The sphere and the cylinder reaches upto maximum heights $h_1$ and $h_2$, respectively, above the initial level. The ratio $h_1: h_2$ is $\frac{n}{10}$. The value of $n$ is______.
solution:
Gain in P.E. = Loss in K.E.
$\mathrm{mgh}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}^2\left(1+\frac{\mathrm{K}^2}{\mathrm{R}^2}\right)$
$\mathrm{h} \propto 1+\frac{\mathrm{K}^2}{\mathrm{R}^2}$
$\frac{\mathrm{h}_1}{\mathrm{~h}_2}=\frac{1+\frac{2}{5}}{1+1}=\frac{7}{5 \times 2}=\frac{7}{10}$
$\mathrm{n}=7$

subject: Physics
q: A body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> is projected with a speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>u</mi></math> making an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>45</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></math> with the ground. The angular momentum of the body about the point of projection, at the highest point is expressed as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mi>m</mi><msup><mi>u</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><mi>X</mi><mi>g</mi></mrow></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math> is_______.
question: A body of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>m</mi></math> is projected with a speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>u</mi></math> making an angle of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>45</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi></msup></math> with the ground. The angular momentum of the body about the point of projection, at the highest point is expressed as <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mrow><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mi>m</mi><msup><mi>u</mi><mn>3</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><mi>X</mi><mi>g</mi></mrow></mfrac></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math> is_______.
solution:
Consider projection as shown in above diagram.

At the topmost point, velocity of the body will be ucos45°.

Therefore, we can write the required angular momentum as 

L=muH×H=mucosθu2sin2θ2g

=mu3×142g⇒X=8

subject: Physics
q: A disc of radius R and mass M is rolling horizontally without slipping with speed v. It then moves up an inclined smooth surface as shown in figure. The maximum height that the disc can go up the incline is:
question: A disc of radius R and mass M is rolling horizontally without slipping with speed v. It then moves up an inclined smooth surface as shown in figure. The maximum height that the disc can go up the incline is:
solution:
Only the translational kinetic energy of disc changes into gravitational potential energy and rotational KE remains unchanged as there is no friction.

12mv2=mgh

⇒h=v22g

subject: Physics
q: A solid spherical ball is rolling on a frictionless horizontal plane surface about its axis of symmetry. The ratio of rotational kinetic energy of the ball to its total kinetic energy is
question: A solid spherical ball is rolling on a frictionless horizontal plane surface about its axis of symmetry. The ratio of rotational kinetic energy of the ball to its total kinetic energy is
solution:
Under pure rolling condition: v=ωR

Translational K.E=12mv2

Rotational K.E=12×25mR2vR2=15mv2

Total K.E=12mv2+15mv2=710mv2

So, Rotational K·ETotal K·E=15mv2710mv2=27

subject: Physics
q: A cylinder is rolling down on an inclined plane of inclination <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math>. Its acceleration during rolling down will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo></math> _______(use <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>).
question: A cylinder is rolling down on an inclined plane of inclination <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math>. Its acceleration during rolling down will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mi>x</mi><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt></mfrac><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>, where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>=</mo></math> _______(use <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>).
solution:
For rolling motion, the acceleration of the cylinder along the inclined plane can be expressed as

a=gsinθ1+IcmMR2   ...1

The moment of inertia of the cylinder can be written as

Icm=12MR2   ...2

From equations (1) and (2), it follows that

a=gsinθ1+12=2×10×323=103 m s-2

Therefore, x=10.

subject: Physics
q: A solid sphere and a hollow sphere of the same mass and of same radius are rolled on an inclined plane. Let the time taken to reach the bottom by the solid sphere and the hollow sphere be $t_1$ and $t_2$, respectively, then
question: A solid sphere and a hollow sphere of the same mass and of same radius are rolled on an inclined plane. Let the time taken to reach the bottom by the solid sphere and the hollow sphere be $t_1$ and $t_2$, respectively, then
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{t}=\sqrt{\frac{2 \ell}{\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{cm}}}} \\ & \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{cm}}=\frac{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}{1+\frac{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{cm}}}{\mathrm{MR}^2}} \\ & \mathrm{a}_1=\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{cm}_1}=\frac{5 \mathrm{~g} \sin \theta}{7} \ldots . . \text { Solid } \\ & \mathrm{a}_2=\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{cm}_2}=\frac{3 \mathrm{~g} \sin \theta}{5} \ldots . . \text { Hollow } \\ & \mathrm{a}_1>\mathrm{a}_2 \\ & \mathrm{t}_1 < \mathrm{t}_2\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: A circular disc has radius $R_{1}$ and thickness $T_{1}$. Another circular disc made of the same material has radius $R_{2}$ and thickness $T_{2}$. If the moment of inertia of both discs are same and $\frac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}=2$ then $\frac{T_{1}}{T_{2}}=\frac{1}{\alpha}$. The value of $\alpha$ is $\_\_\_\_$.
question: A circular disc has radius $R_{1}$ and thickness $T_{1}$. Another circular disc made of the same material has radius $R_{2}$ and thickness $T_{2}$. If the moment of inertia of both discs are same and $\frac{R_{1}}{R_{2}}=2$ then $\frac{T_{1}}{T_{2}}=\frac{1}{\alpha}$. The value of $\alpha$ is $\_\_\_\_$.
solution:
For a circular disc made of material with density $\rho$, moment of inertia is $I = \frac{1}{2}MR^2 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R^2 T \cdot R^2 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R^4 T$.
For disc 1: $I_1 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R_1^4 T_1$
For disc 2: $I_2 = \frac{1}{2}\rho \pi R_2^4 T_2$
Given $I_1 = I_2$:
$R_1^4 T_1 = R_2^4 T_2$
With $\frac{R_1}{R_2} = 2$:
$(2R_2)^4 T_1 = R_2^4 T_2$
$16 R_2^4 T_1 = R_2^4 T_2$
$\frac{T_1}{T_2} = \frac{1}{16}$
Therefore $\alpha = 16$.

subject: Physics
q: The radius of gyration of a cylindrical rod about an axis of rotation perpendicular to its length and passing through the center will be _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Given, the length of the rod is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>.
question: The radius of gyration of a cylindrical rod about an axis of rotation perpendicular to its length and passing through the center will be _____ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>.</mo></math> Given, the length of the rod is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><msqrt><mn>3</mn></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>.
solution:
Moment of inertia of the rod about an axis perpendicular to its length and passing through the centre is I=ml212.

Using the relation, I=mk2, we have

⇒k2=l212⇒k=l12=l23=10323=5 m

subject: Physics
q: Two circular discs of radius each 10 cm are joined at their centres by a rod of length 30 cm and mass 600 gm as shown in figure. If the mass of each disc is 600 gm and applied torque between two discs is $43 \times 10^{5}$ dyne. cm, the angular acceleration of the discs about the given axis $A B$ is $\_\_\_\_$ $\mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$.
question: Two circular discs of radius each 10 cm are joined at their centres by a rod of length 30 cm and mass 600 gm as shown in figure. If the mass of each disc is 600 gm and applied torque between two discs is $43 \times 10^{5}$ dyne. cm, the angular acceleration of the discs about the given axis $A B$ is $\_\_\_\_$ $\mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^{2}$.
solution:
$\alpha = \dfrac{\tau}{I}$
$I = \dfrac{1}{4}mR^2 + mR^2 + \dfrac{1}{4}mR^2 + m(2R)^2 + \dfrac{m(3R)^2}{12} + m\left(\dfrac{R}{2}\right)^2$
$= \left(\dfrac{3}{2} + 4 + 1\right)mR^2 = \dfrac{13}{2}mR^2 = \dfrac{13}{2} \times 600 \times 10^2 = 39 \times 10^4$
$\alpha = \dfrac{43 \times 10^5}{39 \times 10^4} \text{ rad/s}^2 = \dfrac{430}{39} \text{ rad/s}^2 \approx 11 \text{ rad/s}^2$

subject: Physics
q: A disc of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> and radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is free of rotate about a horizontal axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to the plane of disc. A body of same mass as that of disc is fixed at the highest point of the disc. Now the system is released, when the body comes to the lowest position, its angular speed will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msqrt><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mrow></mfrac></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi>rad</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo></math> _____ .
question: A disc of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> and radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> is free of rotate about a horizontal axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to the plane of disc. A body of same mass as that of disc is fixed at the highest point of the disc. Now the system is released, when the body comes to the lowest position, its angular speed will be <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><msqrt><mfrac><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></mrow></mfrac></msqrt><mo> </mo><mi>rad</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> where <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo></math> _____ .
solution:
Let the angular speed of disc be ω.

Using conservation of mechanical energy

mg2R=12Idisc ω2+12Iparticle ω2

Where, I is moment of inertia.

⇒mg2R=ω22m22+mR2

⇒mg2R=ω2232mR2

⇒34ω2=2gR             

 ⇒ω2=8g3R

Thus, angular speed is ω=803R rad s-1

Given ω=4x3R

Comparing both, 16x3R=803R

Therefore, the value of x=5.

q:
Moment of inertia M.I. of four bodies, having same mass and radius, are reported as;

I1=M.I. of thin circular ring about its diameter,

I2=M.I. of circular disc about an axis perpendicular to disc and going through the centre,

I3=M.I. of solid cylinder about its axis and

I4=M.I. of solid sphere about its diameter.

Then:
question:
Moment of inertia M.I. of four bodies, having same mass and radius, are reported as;

I1=M.I. of thin circular ring about its diameter,

I2=M.I. of circular disc about an axis perpendicular to disc and going through the centre,

I3=M.I. of solid cylinder about its axis and

I4=M.I. of solid sphere about its diameter.

Then:
solution:
Ring I1=MR22 about diameter

Disc I2=MR22

Solid cylinder I3=MR22

Solid sphere I4=25MR2

I1=I2=I3>I4

subject: Physics
q: A circular disc of mass <math><mtext>M</mtext></math> and radius <math><mtext>R</mtext></math> is rotating about its axis with angular speed <math><mrow><msub><mi>ω</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mrow></math>. If another stationary disc having radius <math><mrow><mfrac><mtext>R</mtext><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></math> and same mass <math><mtext>M</mtext></math> is dropped co-axially on to the rotating disc. Gradually both discs attain constant angular speed <math><mrow><msub><mi>ω</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></math>. The energy lost in the process is <math><mrow><mtext>p%</mtext></mrow></math> of the initial energy. Value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></math> is ______
question: A circular disc of mass <math><mtext>M</mtext></math> and radius <math><mtext>R</mtext></math> is rotating about its axis with angular speed <math><mrow><msub><mi>ω</mi><mn>1</mn></msub></mrow></math>. If another stationary disc having radius <math><mrow><mfrac><mtext>R</mtext><mn>2</mn></mfrac></mrow></math> and same mass <math><mtext>M</mtext></math> is dropped co-axially on to the rotating disc. Gradually both discs attain constant angular speed <math><mrow><msub><mi>ω</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></math>. The energy lost in the process is <math><mrow><mtext>p%</mtext></mrow></math> of the initial energy. Value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi></math> is ______
solution:
I1ω1+I2ω2=I1+I2×ωf

 

MR22×ω+0MR22+MR281625

ωf=45ω

Final K.E., Kf=12MR22+MR281625ω2

Kf=MR2ω25 ; Ki=12MR22ω2=MR2ω24

Percentage loss in kinetic energy % loss=MR2ω24−MR2ω25MR2ω24×100=20%

subject: Physics
q:
A uniform solid cylinder with radius R and length L has moment of inertia I1, about the axis of cylinder. A concentric solid cylinder of radius R'=R2  and length L'=L2 is carved out of the original cylinder. If I2 is the moment of inertia of the carved out portion of the cylinder then I1I2=  __________.

(Both I1 and I2 are about the axis of the cylinder)
question:
A uniform solid cylinder with radius R and length L has moment of inertia I1, about the axis of cylinder. A concentric solid cylinder of radius R'=R2  and length L'=L2 is carved out of the original cylinder. If I2 is the moment of inertia of the carved out portion of the cylinder then I1I2=  __________.

(Both I1 and I2 are about the axis of the cylinder)
solution:
Moment of inertia of solid cylinder of radius R and length L about the axis is I1=M1R22.

Moment of inertia of carved out portion of the cylinder of radius R2 and length L2 is I2=M2R222=M2R28

Now, the ratio I1I2=4M1M2

As, mass=density×volume

So, I1I2=4×ρπR2LρπR2L4×2=32

subject: Physics
q: A uniform sphere of weight $W$ and radius $5 \mathrm{~cm}$ is being held by a string as shown in the figure. The tension in the string will be :
question: A uniform sphere of weight $W$ and radius $5 \mathrm{~cm}$ is being held by a string as shown in the figure. The tension in the string will be :
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{PQ}=\sqrt{\mathrm{OP}^2+\mathrm{OQ}^2} \\ & =\sqrt{13^2+5^2}=12 \end{aligned}$ Tension in the string $\mathrm{T}$, so, $\mathrm{T} \cos \theta=\mathrm{w}$ $\Rightarrow \mathrm{T}=\frac{\mathrm{w}}{\cos \theta}=\frac{13}{12} \mathrm{~W}$

subject: Physics
q:
A wheel of radius 0.2 m rotates freely about its center when a string that is wrapped over its rim is pulled by force of 10 N as shown in figure. The established torque produces an angular acceleration of $2 \mathrm{~rad} / \mathrm{s}^2$. Moment of inertia of the wheel is _____ $\mathrm{kg} \mathrm{m}^2$.
(Acceleration due to gravity $=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$)
question:
A wheel of radius 0.2 m rotates freely about its center when a string that is wrapped over its rim is pulled by force of 10 N as shown in figure. The established torque produces an angular acceleration of $2 \mathrm{~rad} / \mathrm{s}^2$. Moment of inertia of the wheel is _____ $\mathrm{kg} \mathrm{m}^2$.
(Acceleration due to gravity $=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$)
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{FR}=\mathrm{I} \alpha \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{FR}}{\alpha}=\frac{10 \times 0.2}{2}=1 \mathrm{~kg}-\mathrm{m}^2\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: Two small balls with masses $m$ and 2 m are attached to both ends of a rigid rod of length $d$ and negligible mass. If angular momentum of this system is $L$ about an axis ($A$) passing through its centre of mass and perpendicular to the rod then angular velocity of the system about $A$ is :
question: Two small balls with masses $m$ and 2 m are attached to both ends of a rigid rod of length $d$ and negligible mass. If angular momentum of this system is $L$ about an axis ($A$) passing through its centre of mass and perpendicular to the rod then angular velocity of the system about $A$ is :
solution:
CM from mass $m$: $x_{cm} = \frac{2md}{3m} = \frac{2d}{3}$.
Distances: $m$ is $2d/3$ from CM, $2m$ is $d/3$ from CM.
$I_{cm} = m(2d/3)^2 + 2m(d/3)^2 = \frac{4md^2}{9} + \frac{2md^2}{9} = \frac{2md^2}{3}$.
$L = I\omega \Rightarrow \omega = \frac{L}{I} = \frac{3L}{2md^2}$.

q: A solid sphere of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> rolls without slipping on a plane surface. Its kinetic energy is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>. The speed of the centre of mass of the sphere is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>.
question: A solid sphere of mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> rolls without slipping on a plane surface. Its kinetic energy is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo>×</mo><msup><mn>10</mn><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">J</mi></math>. The speed of the centre of mass of the sphere is ______ <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>cm</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>.
solution:
Let velocity of centre of mass of sphere be v.

Total kinetic energy of solid sphere is KE=12mv2+12Iω2, where, I is moment of inertia of the solid sphere about the axis passing through it's centre of mass and ω=vR pure rolling condition.

Therefore,

 7×10-3=12×1×(v)2+1225(1)R2vR2

⇒12v275=7×10-3

⇒v2=10-2

⇒v=10-1=0.1 m s-1=10 cm s-1

subject: Physics
q: A wheel initially at rest is subjected to a uniform angular acceleration about its axis. In the first $2\text{ s}$ it rotates through an angle $\theta_1$ and in the next $2\text{ s}$ it rotates through an angle $\theta_2$. The ratio $\dfrac{\theta_2}{\theta_1}$ is _______.
question: A wheel initially at rest is subjected to a uniform angular acceleration about its axis. In the first $2\text{ s}$ it rotates through an angle $\theta_1$ and in the next $2\text{ s}$ it rotates through an angle $\theta_2$. The ratio $\dfrac{\theta_2}{\theta_1}$ is _______.
solution:
Using the equation of rotational kinematics $\theta = \omega_0 t + \dfrac{1}{2} \alpha t^2$.

Since the wheel is initially at rest, $\omega_0 = 0$.

Angle rotated in the first $2\text{ s}$:

$\theta_1 = \dfrac{1}{2} \alpha (2)^2 = 2\alpha$

Angle rotated in the first $4\text{ s}$:

$\theta_{total} = \dfrac{1}{2} \alpha (4)^2 = 8\alpha$

Angle rotated in the next $2\text{ s}$:

$\theta_2 = \theta_{total} - \theta_1 = 8\alpha - 2\alpha = 6\alpha$

The ratio is:

$\dfrac{\theta_2}{\theta_1} = \dfrac{6\alpha}{2\alpha} = 3$

Answer: $3$

q: To mop-clean a floor, a cleaning machine presses a circular mop of radius <math><mi>R</mi></math> vertically down with a total force <math><mi>F</mi></math> and rotates it with a constant angular speed about its axis. If the force <math><mi>F</mi></math> is distributed uniformly over the mop and if coefficient of friction between the mop and the floor is <math><mi>μ</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the torque, applied by the machine on the mop is:
question: To mop-clean a floor, a cleaning machine presses a circular mop of radius <math><mi>R</mi></math> vertically down with a total force <math><mi>F</mi></math> and rotates it with a constant angular speed about its axis. If the force <math><mi>F</mi></math> is distributed uniformly over the mop and if coefficient of friction between the mop and the floor is <math><mi>μ</mi><mo>,</mo></math> the torque, applied by the machine on the mop is:
solution:
Net torque =∑Torque on ring

∫dτ=∫0Rμ×FπR2×2πx dxx
τ=2μ3FR

subject: Physics
q:
$\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C are disc, solid sphere and spherical shell respectively with same radii and masses. These masses are placed as shown in figure.

The moment of inertia of the given system about $P Q$ is $\frac{X}{15} I$, where $I$ is the moment of inertia of the disc about its diameter. The value of x is _______.
question:
$\mathrm{A}, \mathrm{B}$ and C are disc, solid sphere and spherical shell respectively with same radii and masses. These masses are placed as shown in figure.

The moment of inertia of the given system about $P Q$ is $\frac{X}{15} I$, where $I$ is the moment of inertia of the disc about its diameter. The value of x is _______.
solution:
All bodies have same mass and same radius.
$\mathrm{A} \rightarrow$ Disc
$\mathrm{B} \rightarrow$ Solid sphere
$\mathrm{C} \rightarrow$ Spherical shell
and, $\mathrm{I}=\frac{\mathrm{MR}^2}{4}$
$\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{PQ}}=\frac{\mathrm{MR}^2}{4}+\left(\frac{2}{5} \mathrm{MR}^2+\mathrm{MR}^2\right)+\left(\frac{2}{3} \mathrm{MR}^2+\mathrm{MR}^2\right)$
$\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{PQ}}=\frac{15 \mathrm{MR}^2+24 \mathrm{MR}^2+60 \mathrm{MR}^2+40 \mathrm{MR}^2+60 \mathrm{MR}^2}{60}$
$\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{PQ}}=\frac{199}{60} \mathrm{MR}^2=\frac{199}{15}\left(\frac{\mathrm{MR}^2}{4}\right)$
$=\frac{199}{15} \mathrm{I}$

q: A solid sphere and a solid cylinder of same mass and radius are rolling on a horizontal surface without slipping. The ratio of their radius of gyrations respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>k</mi><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msub><mo>:</mo><msub><mi>k</mi><mrow><mi>c</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>l</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is _______.
question: A solid sphere and a solid cylinder of same mass and radius are rolling on a horizontal surface without slipping. The ratio of their radius of gyrations respectively <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msub><mi>k</mi><mrow><mi>s</mi><mi>p</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msub><mo>:</mo><msub><mi>k</mi><mrow><mi>c</mi><mi>y</mi><mi>l</mi></mrow></msub></mrow></mfenced></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>:</mo><msqrt><mi>x</mi></msqrt></math>. The value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> is _______.
solution:
Considering rotational axis as the diametrical axis for sphere and axis of cylinder. Then

K12=25R2 and K22=12R2

∴ K1K2=2/51/2=45

⇒K1K2=25

Comparing it with the given value in the question,

25=2x

∴ x=5

subject: Physics
q: A circular disc reaches from top to bottom of an inclined plane of length $l$. When it slips down the plane, if takes $t \mathrm{~s}$. When it rolls down the plane then it takes $\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right)^{1 / 2} t \mathrm{~s}$, where $\alpha$ is _________
question: A circular disc reaches from top to bottom of an inclined plane of length $l$. When it slips down the plane, if takes $t \mathrm{~s}$. When it rolls down the plane then it takes $\left(\frac{\alpha}{2}\right)^{1 / 2} t \mathrm{~s}$, where $\alpha$ is _________
solution:
For slipping
$\begin{aligned}& \mathrm{a}=\mathrm{g} \sin \theta \\
& \ell=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{at}^2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=\sqrt{\frac{2 \ell}{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}}\end{aligned}$
For rolling
$\begin{aligned}& \mathrm{a}^{\prime}=\frac{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}{1+\frac{\mathrm{k}^2}{\mathrm{R}^2}}\left[\mathrm{k}=\frac{\mathrm{R}}{\sqrt{2}}\right] \\& \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}^{\prime}=\frac{2 \mathrm{~g} \sin \theta}{3} \\& \ell=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{a}^{\prime}\left(\mathrm{t}^{\prime}\right)^2 \\& \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}^{\prime}=\sqrt{\frac{6 \ell}{2 \mathrm{~g} \sin \theta}}=\sqrt{\frac{\alpha}{2}} \sqrt{\frac{2 \ell}{\mathrm{g} \sin \theta}} \\
& \Rightarrow \alpha=3\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: A heavy iron bar, of weight $W$ is having its one end on the ground and the other on the shoulder of a person. The bar makes an angle $\theta$ with the horizontal. The weight experienced by the person is :
question: A heavy iron bar, of weight $W$ is having its one end on the ground and the other on the shoulder of a person. The bar makes an angle $\theta$ with the horizontal. The weight experienced by the person is :
solution: $R=$ net reaction force by shoulder Balancing torque about pt of contact on ground: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{W}\left(\frac{\mathrm{L}}{2} \cos \theta\right)=\mathrm{R}(\mathrm{L} \cos \theta) \\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{R}=\frac{\mathrm{W}}{2}\end{aligned}$

subject: Physics
q: The moment of inertia of a uniform cylinder of length <math><mi>l</mi></math> and radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> about its perpendicular bisector is <math><mi>I</mi><mo>.</mo></math> What is the ratio <math><mrow><mrow><mi>l</mi></mrow><mo>/</mo><mrow><mi>R</mi></mrow></mrow></math> such that the moment of inertia is minimum?
question: The moment of inertia of a uniform cylinder of length <math><mi>l</mi></math> and radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math> about its perpendicular bisector is <math><mi>I</mi><mo>.</mo></math> What is the ratio <math><mrow><mrow><mi>l</mi></mrow><mo>/</mo><mrow><mi>R</mi></mrow></mrow></math> such that the moment of inertia is minimum?
solution:
m=ρπR2l  ...(1)

dm=ρπ2R.dRl+R2dl

0=ρπ2RldR+R2dl

2Rl dR=-R2dl  ...(2)

I=MR24+Ml212

I=M12 3R2+l2

dI=M126RdR+2l dl=0  ... (3)

Putting (1), (2) in (3)

6R-R2dl2Rl+2l dl=0

-3R2l+2ldl=0

-3R2+2l2=0

2l2=3R2

lR= 32

subject: Physics
q: A solid sphere is rolling without slipping on a horizontal plane. The ratio of the linear kinetic energy of the centre of mass of the sphere and rotational kinetic energy is :
question: A solid sphere is rolling without slipping on a horizontal plane. The ratio of the linear kinetic energy of the centre of mass of the sphere and rotational kinetic energy is :
solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{KE}_{(T)}=\frac{1}{2} m v^2 \\
& \mathrm{KE}_{(R)}=\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{2}{5} m R^2 \cdot \frac{v^2}{R^2}=\frac{1}{2} m v^2\left(\frac{2}{5}\right)
\end{aligned}$
So, $\frac{\mathrm{KE}_{(T)}}{\mathrm{KE}_{(R)}}=\frac{5}{2}$

q: A heavy iron bar of weight <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is having its one end on the ground and the other on the shoulder of a man. The rod makes an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the horizontal, the normal force applied by the man on bar is:
question: A heavy iron bar of weight <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo> </mo><mi>kg</mi></math> is having its one end on the ground and the other on the shoulder of a man. The rod makes an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>60</mn><mo>°</mo></math> with the horizontal, the normal force applied by the man on bar is:
solution:
Let's consider the following diagram:

For the equilibrium of the system, the net torque, according to the above figure, on the system about point A should be zero.

With reference to the above figure, the net anti-clockwise torque about A is

τa=F2L   ...1

And, the net clockwise torque about the same point is

τc=Wcosθ×L2    ...2

Equations (1) and (2) imply that

Wcosθ×L2=F2L⇒12×10×cos60°×L2=F2 L⇒F2=120×10×cos60°2=30

Hence, the required weight can be expressed as

F2=3 kg-wt

subject: Physics
q: A solid cylinder and a solid sphere, having same mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> and radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math>, roll down the same inclined plane from top without slipping. They start from rest. The ratio of velocity of the solid cylinder to that of the solid sphere, with which they reach the ground, will be
question: A solid cylinder and a solid sphere, having same mass <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>M</mi></math> and radius <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>R</mi></math>, roll down the same inclined plane from top without slipping. They start from rest. The ratio of velocity of the solid cylinder to that of the solid sphere, with which they reach the ground, will be
solution:
During pure rolling, the work done by the friction is zero. Therefore, we can apply conservation of mechanical energy.

mgh=12mv2+12IvR2

⇒mgh=12m+IR2v2

⇒v=2gh1+ImR2

For a solid cylinder ImR2=12 and for a solid sphere, ImR2=25

vCylvSph=1+251+12=1415
